divergence_notes: vs plby + jayyhk (identical defs in both hosted excerpts): egyptianSum, ValidEgyptian, IsUnderapprox, IsBestNTerm, EventuallyGreedy adopted verbatim; plby marks egyptianSum `noncomputable def`, jayyhk plain `def` — ours is `noncomputable` (real division), matching plby.
vs both hosted: harmonicNumber and X_set are omitted — they are scaffolding for Kovač's measure-zero proof (the X_{s,t} covering sets), not part of the question statement.

vs both hosted: the hosted excerpts truncate before their main theorem, so the top-level form is ours: FC house question-form `answer(False) ↔ ∀ᵐ x ∂(volume.restrict (Set.Ioi 0)), EventuallyGreedy x` (disproved upstream state; Kovač shows the eventually-greedy set has Lebesgue measure zero, so the almost-all claim is false).

vs problem text: the greedy recursion R_{n+1}=R_n+1/m (m minimal unused with RHS < x) is rendered as the hosted nested-prefix form (EventuallyGreedy); equivalence argued in the Formalization notes block of 206.lean — a nested chain of best sets forces the appended denominator to be the minimal admissible one, and conversely.
